recipes.name: Grilled Chicken Quinoa Power Bowl
steps: Grill seasoned chicken breast until cooked through. Cook quinoa according to package directions.
Steam broccoli until tender-crisp. Halve cherry tomatoes.
Assemble bowl with quinoa, sliced chicken, broccoli, and tomatoes. Drizzle with olive oil and lemon.
recipes.tip: Aim for lean protein sources; they keep you full longer, powering your day.

recipes.name: Salmon, Asparagus, Sweet Potato Medley
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Toss diced sweet potato with 1 tbsp olive oil and roast for 20 minutes.
Add asparagus and salmon (seasoned with salt, pepper, garlic powder) to the baking sheet. Drizzle remaining olive oil.
Bake for another 10-15 minutes until salmon is flaky. Garnish with fresh dill and serve.
recipes.tip: Omega-3s are vital! Incorporate fatty fish weekly for heart health and satiety.

recipes.name: Lean Beef Broccoli Stir-fry
steps: Heat a wok or large skillet. Stir-fry sliced beef until browned, remove from pan.
Add broccoli and bell pepper to the pan, stir-fry until tender-crisp.
Return beef to pan. Add soy sauce and ginger, toss to coat. Serve immediately over brown rice.
recipes.tip: Prep veggies ahead for quick weeknight meals. No excuses for missing your protein!

recipes.name: Hearty Tofu Veggie Scramble
steps: Heat a skillet with a little oil. Sauté onion and bell pepper until softened.
Crumble pressed tofu directly into the pan. Add spinach, nutritional yeast, turmeric, salt, and pepper.
Cook, stirring occasionally, until tofu is heated through and spinach wilts. Serve with whole-wheat toast.
recipes.tip: Plant-based protein sources are versatile and nutrient-dense. Discover new favorites!

recipes.name: High-Protein Chicken Salad Wrap
steps: In a bowl, combine shredded chicken, Greek yogurt, diced celery, and halved red grapes. Mix well.
Season the chicken salad with salt and pepper to taste.
Lay out whole-wheat tortillas. Place lettuce leaves on each, then fill with the chicken salad. Roll up tightly and enjoy.
recipes.tip: Swap mayo for Greek yogurt to boost protein and cut unhealthy fats without sacrificing flavor.

recipes.name: Spicy Lentil Spinach Detox Soup
steps: In a large pot, sauté diced onion and minced garlic until fragrant. Add cumin and chili flakes, cook for 1 minute.
Stir in red lentils, vegetable broth, and diced tomatoes. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 25-30 minutes, until lentils are tender.
Stir in fresh spinach until wilted. Season with salt and pepper to taste before serving.
recipes.tip: Fiber-rich lentils help regulate blood sugar and aid digestion. A must for any plan!

recipes.name: Turkey Zucchini Meatloaf Muffins
steps: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Lightly grease a 12-cup muffin tin.
In a large bowl, combine ground turkey, shredded zucchini, egg, almond flour, onion powder, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
Divide mixture evenly into muffin cups. Top each with a small amount of ketchup. Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until cooked through.
recipes.tip: Portion control is key. These muffins make meal prepping simple and satisfying!

recipes.name: Shrimp Cauliflower Rice Bowl
steps: Heat a large skillet or wok with sesame oil. Add shrimp and cook until pink, remove from pan.
Add bell peppers and snow peas to the pan, stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until tender-crisp.
Add cauliflower rice and cooked shrimp back to the pan. Drizzle with soy sauce and stir-fry for another 3-5 minutes until heated through.
recipes.tip: Cauliflower rice is a great low-carb swap for traditional grains, boosting your veggie intake.

recipes.name: Cottage Cheese Berry Power Bowl
steps: Spoon cottage cheese into a bowl. Gently rinse and drain the mixed berries.
Arrange the mixed berries on top of the cottage cheese.
Sprinkle with slivered almonds and drizzle lightly with honey before serving.
recipes.tip: Cottage cheese is a fantastic source of casein protein for slow release and lasting fullness.

recipes.name: Chicken Veggie Skewers Hummus
steps: Preheat grill or oven to 400°F (200°C). Thread chicken cubes and vegetables onto skewers.
Brush skewers lightly with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Grill or bake for 15-20 minutes, turning occasionally, until chicken is cooked through. Serve with hummus and warm pita.
recipes.tip: Grilling is a lean cooking method that locks in flavor naturally. Enjoy that char!

recipes.name: Black Bean Corn Stuffed Peppers
steps: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Halve bell peppers lengthwise and remove seeds.
In a bowl, combine cooked quinoa, rinsed black beans, corn, diced onion, cilantro, and chili powder. Mix well.
Stuff the pepper halves generously with the mixture. Place in a baking dish with a splash of water. Bake for 30-35 minutes until peppers are tender. Serve with salsa.
recipes.tip: Combining plant proteins like beans and quinoa makes a complete protein. Smart move!

recipes.name: Spinach Feta Egg White Omelet
steps: Heat olive oil in a non-stick skillet over medium heat. Sauté spinach and cherry tomatoes until spinach wilts.
Whisk egg whites with a pinch of salt and pepper. Pour into the skillet over the vegetables.
Cook until edges set, then sprinkle with feta cheese. Fold the omelet in half and cook for another 1-2 minutes until cheese is melted.
recipes.tip: Egg whites are pure protein, low in calories and fat. A great way to start!

recipes.name: Seared Tuna Green Bean Plate
steps: Pat tuna steak dry and season generously with salt and pepper. Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a skillet over high heat.
Sear tuna for 1-2 minutes per side for rare/medium-rare. Adjust time for desired doneness. Remove tuna and set aside.
Add remaining 1/2 tbsp olive oil and minced garlic to the pan. Add green beans and sauté until tender-crisp. Serve tuna and beans with lemon wedges.
recipes.tip: Tuna is excellent for lean protein and essential minerals. Don't overcook it!

recipes.name: Pork Tenderloin Roasted Veggies
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Toss chopped carrots, parsnips, and sweet potato with 1 tbsp olive oil, rosemary, salt, and pepper.
Season pork tenderloin with garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Heat remaining 1 tbsp olive oil in an oven-safe skillet and sear pork on all sides.
Transfer vegetables to the skillet around the pork (or to a separate baking sheet). Roast for 20-25 minutes, or until pork reaches 145°F (63°C) and vegetables are tender.
recipes.tip: Lean pork tenderloin is a surprisingly good protein source. Always watch your portion sizes!

recipes.name: Chickpea Vegetable Coconut Curry
steps: Sauté diced onion in a pot until softened. Add red curry paste and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in light coconut milk, rinsed chickpeas, and mixed vegetables. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Cover and cook for 15-20 minutes, or until vegetables are tender. Serve hot over cooked brown rice.
recipes.tip: Batch cook grains like brown rice for easy meal assembly all week. Stay consistent!

recipes.name: Protein Overnight Oats Jar
steps: In a jar or container, combine rolled oats, Greek yogurt, protein powder, chia seeds, and almond milk.
Stir all ingredients thoroughly until well combined and no dry spots remain.
Cover and refrigerate overnight (or at least 4 hours). Top with fresh mixed berries before enjoying.
recipes.tip: Prepping breakfast saves time and helps you hit goals from morning. Win the day!

recipes.name: Lemon Herb Chicken with Asparagus
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a baking sheet.
Toss chicken and asparagus with olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, herbs, salt, and pepper.
Spread on baking sheet and roast for 20-25 minutes, or until cooked through.
recipes.tip: Lean protein builds muscle and boosts metabolism. Focus on quality.

recipes.name: Garlic Butter Salmon with Broccoli
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Arrange salmon and broccoli on a baking sheet.
Melt butter, mix with minced garlic and lemon juice. Drizzle over salmon and broccoli.
Season with salt and pepper. Bake for 15-20 minutes, until salmon is flaky.
recipes.tip: Omega-3s from salmon are fantastic for brain and heart health. Fuel wisely.

recipes.name: Ground Turkey Zucchini Skillet
steps: Heat olive oil in a large skillet. Sauté onion and garlic until fragrant.
Add ground turkey and cook, breaking it apart, until browned. Drain any excess fat.
Stir in zucchini, diced tomatoes, and Italian seasoning. Cook until zucchini is tender-crisp.
recipes.tip: Maximize veggie intake for nutrients and fiber. Aim for color variety.

recipes.name: Steak Salad with Avocado Dressing
steps: Season steak with salt and pepper. Cook to desired doneness, then slice thinly.
Combine avocado, olive oil, and lime juice for dressing. Mash until smooth.
Toss mixed greens, tomatoes, and red onion. Top with sliced steak and avocado dressing.
recipes.tip: Healthy fats like avocado boost satiety and nutrient absorption. Don't fear them!

recipes.name: Shrimp Scampi with Zucchini Noodles
steps: Melt butter in a large skillet. Add garlic and cook until fragrant.
Add shrimp and cook until pink. Deglaze with white wine or broth, then stir in lemon juice.
Add zoodles to the skillet and toss quickly until just tender. Garnish with fresh parsley.
recipes.tip: Zucchini noodles are a great low-carb pasta alternative. Spiralize for success!

recipes.name: Chicken Caesar Lettuce Wraps
steps: In a bowl, combine shredded chicken, low-carb Caesar dressing, Parmesan cheese, and black pepper.
Gently toss until chicken is well coated. Add cherry tomatoes.
Spoon the chicken mixture into large romaine lettuce leaves and serve immediately.
recipes.tip: Prioritize whole foods. Make your own dressing for ultimate ingredient control.

recipes.name: Tofu Scramble with Spinach
steps: Heat olive oil in a non-stick skillet. Sauté onion and bell pepper until softened.
Add crumbled tofu, turmeric, nutritional yeast, and black salt. Cook, stirring, for 5-7 minutes.
Stir in fresh spinach and cook until wilted. Serve hot.
recipes.tip: Plant-based protein is powerful! Tofu is versatile and nutrient-dense.

recipes.name: Pork Chops with Creamy Mushroom Sauce
steps: Season pork chops with salt and pepper. Sear in olive oil until golden brown and cooked through.
Remove chops. Add mushrooms and garlic to the skillet, cook until softened.
Stir in broth, cream, and thyme. Simmer until sauce thickens. Return chops to coat and serve.
recipes.tip: Enjoy rich flavors without excessive carbs. Focus on nutrient-dense ingredients.

recipes.name: Egg & Veggie Breakfast Muffins
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease a 6-cup muffin tin.
Whisk eggs and almond milk with salt and pepper. Stir in bell pepper, spinach, and onion.
Pour mixture into muffin cups. Bake for 20-25 minutes until set and golden.
recipes.tip: Meal prep these for quick, protein-packed breakfasts. Stay fueled all day.

recipes.name: Cod with Mediterranean Salsa
steps: Season cod with salt and pepper. Bake or pan-sear until flaky and cooked through.
Combine tomatoes, cucumber, olives, red onion, olive oil, lemon juice, and parsley for salsa.
Serve cooked cod fillets topped generously with the fresh Mediterranean salsa.
recipes.tip: Lean white fish is excellent for protein without heavy fats. Eat light, feel great.

recipes.name: Spicy Beef Bell Pepper Stir-fry
steps: Heat sesame oil in a large skillet or wok. Stir-fry beef until browned, then remove from pan.
Add bell peppers, onion, garlic, and ginger to the pan. Stir-fry until tender-crisp.
Return beef to pan. Add tamari and sriracha. Toss to coat and heat through. Serve immediately.
recipes.tip: Spice up your meals to boost metabolism and flavor. Keep it exciting!

recipes.name: Turkey Meatballs with Marinara
steps: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). In a bowl, combine turkey, egg, almond flour, garlic powder, and Italian seasoning.
Form into 12-15 meatballs. Brown lightly in olive oil in an oven-safe skillet.
Pour marinara sauce over meatballs. Bake for 20-25 minutes until cooked through.
recipes.tip: Make extra meatballs for easy meal prep. Always have healthy options ready.

recipes.name: Tempeh Broccoli Ginger Stir-fry
steps: Heat sesame oil in a large wok or skillet. Add tempeh and cook until golden brown on all sides.
Add broccoli, carrot, ginger, and garlic. Stir-fry for 5-7 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp.
Stir in tamari and rice vinegar. Toss to coat and heat through. Serve hot.
recipes.tip: Tempeh is a fantastic fermented soy protein source. Great for gut health too!

recipes.name: Chicken Thighs with Braised Greens
steps: Cook bacon until crispy, remove and crumble. Sear chicken thighs in bacon fat (or olive oil) until browned.
Add onion and garlic to the pan, cook until softened. Stir in chopped greens, broth, and vinegar.
Cover and simmer until greens are tender (15-20 mins). Serve chicken with braised greens, topped with crumbled bacon.
recipes.tip: Dark leafy greens are nutrient powerhouses. Don't skip your veggies!

recipes.name: Naked Bunless Burgers with Onions
steps: Form ground beef into a patty. Season with salt and pepper. Cook to desired doneness.
While burger cooks, sauté sliced onion in olive oil until caramelized and tender.
Serve burger patty on lettuce leaves, topped with sautéed onions, tomato, and pickles.
recipes.tip: Choosing higher fat ground beef adds flavor and satiety. Balance with lean options.

recipes.name: Halloumi Vegetable Skewers
steps: Preheat grill or oven to 400°F (200°C). Thread halloumi and vegetables onto skewers.
Drizzle skewers with olive oil, oregano, salt, and pepper, tossing to coat.
Grill or roast for 15-20 minutes, turning occasionally, until vegetables are tender and halloumi is golden.
recipes.tip: Get creative with vegetarian protein! Halloumi offers great texture and flavor.

recipes.name: Grilled Salmon Lemon-Dill Quinoa
steps: Season salmon with salt, pepper, olive oil, and lemon juice; grill or pan-sear until cooked through.
Cook quinoa according to package directions.
Fluff quinoa, then mix with chopped dill, diced cucumber, cherry tomatoes, a squeeze of lemon, and a drizzle of olive oil.
Serve grilled salmon over the lemon-dill quinoa.
recipes.tip: Swap quinoa for cauli-rice to lower carbs, or add Kalamata olives for extra Mediterranean flair!

recipes.name: Chicken Souvlaki Skewers Tzatziki
steps: Marinate chicken cubes in olive oil, lemon juice, oregano, garlic, salt, and pepper for 20 minutes.
Thread chicken onto skewers and grill or broil until cooked through.
Mix grated cucumber, minced garlic, a squeeze of lemon, and salt into Greek yogurt for tzatziki.
Serve chicken souvlaki with fresh tzatziki.
recipes.tip: Add cherry tomatoes and bell peppers to skewers for extra veggies and color!

recipes.name: Mediterranean Lentil Salad Feta
steps: Cook lentils according to package directions until tender, then drain and let cool slightly.
In a large bowl, combine cooked lentils, crumbled feta, cherry tomatoes, cucumber, red onion, and fresh parsley.
Whisk together olive oil, red wine vinegar, salt, and pepper; pour over the salad and toss to combine.
Serve chilled or at room temperature.
recipes.tip: For a vegan option, skip feta and add sun-dried tomatoes or toasted walnuts for richness.

recipes.name: Mediterranean Chickpea Veggie Bowl
steps: In a large bowl, combine chickpeas, cucumber, cherry tomatoes, red onion, Kalamata olives, bell pepper, and fresh parsley.
In a small bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Pour dressing over the chickpea mixture and toss gently to coat.
Serve immediately or chill for later.
recipes.tip: Add a dollop of hummus or a sprinkle of toasted pumpkin seeds for extra texture and flavor.

recipes.name: Shrimp Zucchini Noodle Scampi
steps: Spiralize zucchini into noodles; set aside.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet, add garlic and red pepper flakes; sauté shrimp until pink and cooked through, about 2-3 minutes.
Add zucchini noodles and cherry tomatoes to the skillet, toss gently for 1-2 minutes until zucchini softens slightly.
Stir in fresh parsley and a squeeze of lemon juice. Serve immediately.
recipes.tip: Don't overcook the zucchini noodles! They should be al dente for best texture.

recipes.name: Lamb Kofta Cucumber Raita
steps: In a bowl, mix ground lamb with minced garlic, cumin, coriander, salt, and pepper. Form into small sausage shapes (kofta).
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat and cook kofta until browned on all sides and cooked through.
For raita, mix Greek yogurt with grated cucumber, chopped mint, and a pinch of salt.
Serve hot lamb kofta with cool cucumber raita.
recipes.tip: Serve with a side of mixed greens or grilled bell peppers for a complete meal.

recipes.name: Halloumi Roasted Vegetable Skewers
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Thread halloumi, cherry tomatoes, zucchini, bell pepper, and red onion onto skewers.
Drizzle skewers with olive oil, sprinkle with oregano, salt, and pepper.
Roast for 20-25 minutes, turning halfway, until vegetables are tender-crisp and halloumi is golden.
Finish with a squeeze of lemon juice before serving.
recipes.tip: Marinate veggies and halloumi for an hour before roasting to infuse more flavor.

recipes.name: Tuna Nicoise Style Salad
steps: Blanch green beans in boiling water for 3-4 minutes until tender-crisp; immediately transfer to an ice bath.
Arrange tuna, hard-boiled eggs, green beans, cherry tomatoes, cucumber, and Kalamata olives on a bed of lettuce.
Whisk together olive oil, red wine vinegar, Dijon mustard, salt, and pepper for the dressing.
Drizzle dressing over the salad and serve.
recipes.tip: Use fresh grilled tuna instead of canned for an elevated flavor and texture.

recipes.name: Spiced Cod White Beans Greens
steps: Season cod with smoked paprika, salt, and pepper. Pan-sear in 1 tbsp olive oil until flaky and cooked through.
In the same pan, sauté minced garlic for 1 minute. Add cannellini beans, cherry tomatoes, and the remaining olive oil.
Stir in fresh spinach and cook until wilted. Squeeze fresh lemon juice over the mixture.
Serve the spiced cod over the white bean and greens mixture.
recipes.tip: Add a splash of vegetable broth to the beans and greens for extra moisture and flavor.

recipes.name: Deconstructed Eggplant Moussaka Bowl
steps: Roast diced eggplant with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper at 400°F (200°C) for 20-25 minutes until tender.
While eggplant roasts, cook lentils. Sauté onion and garlic in 1 tbsp olive oil, add crushed tomatoes, cooked lentils, oregano, cinnamon, salt, and pepper; simmer for 15 minutes.
Combine roasted eggplant with the lentil-tomato sauce.
Serve warm, topped with crumbled feta if desired.
recipes.tip: For a vegan option, simply omit the feta cheese and enjoy this hearty plant-based meal.

recipes.name: Greek Lemon Chicken Soup
steps: Bring chicken broth to a simmer. Add shredded chicken and heat through.
In a separate bowl, whisk eggs vigorously, then slowly stream in lemon juice while continuing to whisk.
Gradually temper the egg-lemon mixture by slowly adding 1 cup of hot broth to it while whisking constantly.
Pour the tempered egg mixture back into the pot with the remaining broth and chicken, stirring constantly over low heat until soup thickens slightly. Do not boil.
Stir in fresh dill, olive oil, salt, and pepper. Serve immediately.
recipes.tip: For extra creaminess without dairy, add a splash of unsweetened almond milk to the broth.

recipes.name: Mediterranean Stuffed Bell Peppers
steps: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Lightly oil bell pepper halves and place cut-side up in a baking dish.
Sauté onion and garlic in olive oil. Stir in cooked quinoa, diced tomatoes, chickpeas, parsley, dried mint, salt, and pepper.
Fill bell pepper halves with the quinoa mixture. Add a splash of water to the bottom of the baking dish.
Bake for 30-35 minutes until peppers are tender and filling is heated through.
recipes.tip: Experiment with different herbs like oregano or basil to change up the flavor profile!

recipes.name: Spiced Lamb Chickpea Stew
steps: Heat olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven. Brown lamb stew meat on all sides. Remove lamb.
Sauté onion and garlic until softened. Stir in cumin, smoked paprika, and coriander; cook for 1 minute.
Return lamb to the pot. Add diced tomatoes, chickpeas, and beef broth. Bring to a simmer.
Cover and cook for 45-50 minutes, or until lamb is tender. Stir in fresh parsley before serving.
recipes.tip: Add some diced carrots or zucchini during simmering for extra vitamins and texture.

recipes.name: Baked Falafel Tahini Salad
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). In a food processor, combine chickpeas, parsley, cilantro, onion, garlic, cumin, salt, and pepper. Pulse until well combined but still chunky. Form into small patties or balls.
Arrange falafel on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Drizzle with 1 tbsp olive oil. Bake for 20-25 minutes, flipping halfway, until golden brown.
Whisk together tahini, lemon juice, 1 tbsp olive oil, and a few tablespoons of water until smooth for the dressing. Season with salt.
Serve baked falafel over a bed of lettuce with cucumber, tomato, and drizzle with tahini dressing.
recipes.tip: Don't over-process the falafel mixture; a slightly coarse texture works best for baking.

recipes.name: Pan-Seared Halibut Olive Tapenade
steps: Pat halibut dry, season with salt and pepper. Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Sear halibut for 4-5 minutes per side, until cooked through and flaky.
While halibut cooks, finely chop pitted Kalamata olives, capers, minced garlic, and fresh parsley for the tapenade.
In a small bowl, combine chopped ingredients with 2 tbsp olive oil and a squeeze of lemon juice.
Serve pan-seared halibut topped with the olive tapenade.
recipes.tip: Serve with a simple side salad of mixed greens to complete this flavorful meal.

recipes.name: Spinach Artichoke Frittata
steps: Preheat oven broiler. Heat olive oil in an oven-safe, non-stick skillet. Sauté red onion until softened.
Add spinach and chopped artichoke hearts; cook until spinach wilts. Stir in cherry tomatoes.
In a bowl, whisk eggs with salt and pepper. Pour egg mixture into the skillet over the vegetables. Sprinkle with feta cheese.
Cook on stovetop over medium-low heat for 5-7 minutes until edges are set. Transfer skillet to broiler and cook for 2-3 minutes until top is golden and frittata is puffed.
recipes.tip: Frittata is excellent for meal prep; enjoy it cold or reheated for quick breakfasts.

recipes.name: High-Protein Tofu Scramble Bowl
steps: Press tofu for 10 minutes, then crumble into a bowl. Season with turmeric, black salt, and nutritional yeast.
Heat olive oil in a pan. Sauté diced onion and bell pepper for 5 minutes until softened.
Add crumbled tofu to the pan and cook for 7-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until slightly browned.
Stir in spinach and cook until wilted. Serve immediately.
recipes.tip: Swap bell peppers for mushrooms for an umami boost. Focus on satiety with protein.

recipes.name: Spicy Peanut Tempeh Stir-fry
steps: Slice tempeh into thin strips. Steam or boil tempeh for 5 minutes to soften, then pat dry.
Whisk together peanut butter, soy sauce, rice vinegar, sriracha, and ginger for the sauce.
Heat sesame oil in a large skillet. Add tempeh and cook until golden brown, about 5-7 minutes.
Add broccoli and bell pepper to the skillet, stir-fry for 5 minutes until tender-crisp. Pour in sauce and toss to coat, cooking for 2 minutes until thickened.
recipes.tip: Add a sprinkle of chopped peanuts for extra crunch and healthy fats!

recipes.name: Mediterranean Lentil Salad
steps: Cook lentils according to package instructions if not pre-cooked. Drain and let cool.
In a large bowl, combine cooked lentils, cucumber, cherry tomatoes, red onion, and parsley.
Whisk together lemon juice and olive oil. Pour dressing over the salad.
Add Kalamata olives, toss well, and season with salt and pepper to taste. Chill before serving.
recipes.tip: Boost protein by adding roasted chickpeas. Meal prep this for quick healthy lunches.

recipes.name: Black Bean & Avocado Lettuce Wraps
steps: In a bowl, gently mash about half of the black beans, leaving the rest whole.
Add diced avocado, minced red onion, chopped cilantro, lime juice, cumin, and chili powder to the bowl.
Mix all ingredients until well combined. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Spoon the black bean mixture into individual lettuce leaves and serve.
recipes.tip: For more protein, add some crumbled firm tofu to the black bean mixture!

recipes.name: High-Protein Cauliflower Tabbouleh
steps: Pulse cauliflower florets in a food processor until they resemble couscous. Press water from crumbled tofu.
In a large bowl, combine riced cauliflower, crumbled tofu, parsley, mint, cucumber, and tomatoes.
In a small bowl, whisk together lemon juice and olive oil. Pour dressing over the salad.
Toss everything gently to combine. Season with salt and pepper. Serve immediately or chill.
recipes.tip: This low-carb twist is refreshing! Add toasted pumpkin seeds for extra crunch.

recipes.name: Seitan & Veggie Fajita Bowl
steps: Heat olive oil in a large skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat.
Add sliced bell peppers and onion. Sauté for 5-7 minutes until they begin to soften.
Add seitan and fajita seasoning to the pan. Cook for another 5-8 minutes, stirring frequently, until seitan is browned and vegetables are tender-crisp.
Serve the fajita mixture in bowls, topped with salsa and avocado slices.
recipes.tip: Kick up the flavor with a squeeze of lime juice! It brightens every bite.

recipes.name: Edamame & Green Power Bowl
steps: Cook edamame according to package instructions. Drain and let cool slightly.
In a small bowl, whisk together tahini, lemon juice, minced garlic, and 2 tbsp water for a dressing. Season with salt and pepper.
In a large bowl, combine mixed greens, cooked edamame, diced avocado, and sliced cucumber.
Drizzle dressing over the bowl ingredients. Toss gently to coat and sprinkle with pumpkin seeds before serving.
recipes.tip: Add a dash of tamari to your dressing for extra umami! Power through your day.

recipes.name: Spicy White Bean Skillet
steps: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Sauté diced onion until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add minced garlic and chili flakes, cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in diced tomatoes, vegetable broth, and cannellini beans. Bring to a simmer.
Add chopped kale, stir until wilted, about 5-7 minutes. Season with salt and pepper. Serve warm.
recipes.tip: For higher protein, stir in some plant-based sausage. Fuel your body right.

recipes.name: Mushroom Walnut 'Meat' Lettuce Tacos
steps: Pulse mushrooms and walnuts in a food processor until finely chopped, resembling ground meat.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet. Sauté diced onion and minced garlic for 3-4 minutes.
Add the mushroom-walnut mixture, chili powder, cumin, and smoked paprika. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 10-12 minutes until browned and fragrant.
Season with salt and pepper. Serve mixture in lettuce cups with your favorite toppings.
recipes.tip: Add a dollop of vegan sour cream or avocado crema for extra creaminess!

recipes.name: Hearty Chickpea Curry
steps: Heat olive oil in a large pot. Sauté diced onion until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger, cook for 1 minute. Stir in curry powder and cook for another minute.
Add diced tomatoes, chickpeas, and light coconut milk. Bring to a simmer and cook for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in spinach until wilted. Season with salt and pepper. Serve warm.
recipes.tip: Boost protein with a side of steamed quinoa. Eating healthy doesn't mean boring!

recipes.name: Creamy Vegan Tomato Pasta
steps: Cook pasta according to package directions. Drain and set aside, reserving 1/2 cup pasta water.
Blend soaked cashews, nutritional yeast, garlic, and vegetable broth until smooth and creamy.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet. Add crushed tomatoes, bring to a simmer, then stir in the cashew cream.
Add cooked pasta to the sauce, tossing to coat. If needed, add reserved pasta water to reach desired consistency. Stir in fresh basil.
recipes.tip: Soak cashews for at least 30 minutes for a truly silky smooth sauce. Maximize nutrient density.

recipes.name: Protein-Packed Quinoa Salad
steps: Cook quinoa and lentils separately according to package instructions. Let cool.
In a large bowl, combine cooled quinoa, lentils, diced cucumber, bell pepper, cherry tomatoes, and parsley.
Whisk together lemon juice and olive oil for the dressing. Season with salt and pepper.
Pour dressing over the salad and toss gently to combine. Serve chilled or at room temperature.
recipes.tip: For an extra protein punch, add some chopped plant-based feta! Achieve your goals.

recipes.name: Roasted Chickpea & Veggie Bowl
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Toss chickpeas, broccoli, sweet potato, and red onion with olive oil, paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Spread vegetables in a single layer on a baking sheet. Roast for 20-25 minutes, flipping halfway, until tender and lightly browned.
Serve the roasted vegetables and chickpeas in a bowl, drizzled with tahini lemon dressing.
recipes.tip: Vary your veggies with seasonal options. This helps keep your nutrition exciting!

recipes.name: Spicy Tofu Bánh Mì Bowl
steps: Whisk together sriracha, soy sauce, rice vinegar, and sesame oil for the marinade. Marinate tofu slices for 10 minutes.
Cook rice vermicelli according to package directions. Drain and rinse with cold water.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Cook marinated tofu slices for 3-4 minutes per side until golden and slightly crispy.
Assemble bowls with vermicelli, cooked tofu, julienned carrot and cucumber, sliced radishes, and fresh cilantro. Drizzle with extra marinade if desired.
recipes.tip: Pickle your veggies for extra tang and probiotics! Variety is the spice of life.

recipes.name: Savory Southwest Tofu Scramble
steps: Press tofu for 10 minutes, then crumble into a bowl. Season with chili powder, cumin, salt, and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a skillet. Sauté diced red onion and green bell pepper until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add crumbled tofu to the pan and cook for 7-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until slightly browned.
Stir in black beans, corn, and salsa. Cook for 3-5 minutes until heated through. Serve immediately.
recipes.tip: Top with fresh avocado slices for healthy fats. Make every meal count!

recipes.name: Spiced Lentil Patties with Greens

ingredients.name: Flax egg (1 tbsp ground flax + 3 tbsp water)

steps: In a bowl, mash cooked lentils until mostly smooth. Stir in oat flour, flax egg, diced red onion, minced garlic, cumin, coriander, salt, and pepper.
Form the mixture into 4-5 patties. Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Cook patties for 4-5 minutes per side, until golden brown and firm. Add more oil if needed.
Serve patties over mixed greens with a light vinaigrette or lemon squeeze.
recipes.tip: For crispy patties, bake or air-fry them! Make healthy choices achievable.

recipes.name: Creamy Avocado Zucchini Noodles
steps: In a blender, combine avocado, cashews, lemon juice, garlic, nutritional yeast, salt, pepper, and 1/4 cup water. Blend until smooth and creamy.
Gently warm the zucchini noodles in a pan for 1-2 minutes or serve raw.
Toss the zucchini noodles with the creamy avocado sauce.
Stir in halved cherry tomatoes and fresh basil. Serve immediately.
recipes.tip: Boost protein with a side of pan-fried tempeh cubes. Fuel your body, feel good!

recipes.name: Spicy Black Bean & Corn Soup
steps: Heat olive oil in a large pot. Sauté diced onion until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add minced garlic, chili powder, and cumin, cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in black beans, corn, diced tomatoes, and vegetable broth. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper. For a thicker soup, mash some of the beans against the side of the pot.
recipes.tip: Serve with a dollop of vegan sour cream or fresh cilantro. Eat well, live well.

recipes.name: Tofu Scramble Breakfast Burrito
steps: Press tofu for 10 minutes, then crumble into a bowl. Season with turmeric, black salt, and nutritional yeast.
Heat olive oil in a pan. Sauté diced bell pepper for 3 minutes until softened.
Add crumbled tofu to the pan and cook for 5-7 minutes until slightly browned. Stir in spinach until wilted.
Warm tortilla. Fill with tofu scramble, top with salsa, wrap, and enjoy.
recipes.tip: Add black beans for extra fiber and protein. Smart choices lead to results.

recipes.name: High-Protein Lentil Shepherd's Pie
steps: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Peel and cube sweet potatoes, boil until tender, then mash with a splash of plant milk, salt, and pepper.
Heat olive oil in an oven-safe skillet. Sauté diced onion, carrots, and celery for 5-7 minutes.
Stir in cooked lentils, vegetable broth, tomato paste, and thyme. Simmer for 10 minutes until slightly thickened. Season with salt and pepper.
Spread mashed sweet potato evenly over the lentil mixture. Bake for 20-25 minutes until golden and bubbly.
recipes.tip: Add some frozen peas for extra green goodness. Healthy eating is a lifestyle.

recipes.name: Chickpea & Spinach Coconut Curry
steps: Sauté chopped onion, garlic, and ginger until fragrant in a pot.
Stir in curry paste, then coconut milk, chickpeas, and a splash of water. Simmer for 15 minutes.
Add spinach, cooking until wilted. Serve hot over cooked basmati rice.
recipes.tip: Boost protein with a side of lentils. Fuel your day, plant by plant!

recipes.name: Hearty Lentil Shepherd's Pie
steps: Boil potatoes until tender, then mash with a splash of broth and nutritional yeast.
Sauté lentils with mixed veggies, tomato paste, and thyme. Add broth and simmer until lentils are tender.
Transfer lentil mixture to a baking dish, top with mashed potatoes, and bake at 375°F (190°C) for 25 minutes.
recipes.tip: Add a sprinkle of smoked paprika for extra depth. Comfort food can be healthy!

recipes.name: Black Bean Burgers with Avocado
steps: Mash black beans, then combine with oats, diced bell pepper, onion, and paprika.
Form into two patties and bake or pan-fry until golden brown and firm.
Serve patties on whole wheat buns with sliced avocado and your favorite toppings.
recipes.tip: Add a flax egg for extra binding power. Enjoy guilt-free burger nights!

recipes.name: Tofu Scramble Power Bowl
steps: Cube and roast sweet potato until tender. Crumble tofu and sauté with bell pepper.
Add turmeric and nutritional yeast to tofu, stirring well. Cook until heated through.
Stir in spinach until wilted. Assemble in a bowl with roasted sweet potato and avocado slices.
recipes.tip: Meal prep this for quick, protein-packed breakfasts all week. Consistency is key!

recipes.name: Spinach & Feta Stuffed Portobellos
steps: Brush mushroom caps with olive oil. Sauté spinach and garlic until wilted.
Mix spinach with feta cheese and breadcrumbs. Stuff mixture into mushroom caps.
Bake at 400°F (200°C) for 20-25 minutes until mushrooms are tender and filling is golden.
recipes.tip: For lower carbs, skip the breadcrumbs. Focus on nutrient-dense veggies!

recipes.name: Mediterranean Quinoa Salad
steps: Cook quinoa according to package directions. Let cool completely.
Combine cooled quinoa with cucumber, tomatoes, olives, red onion, and parsley.
Whisk together lemon juice and olive oil. Pour dressing over salad and toss to combine.
recipes.tip: Add canned chickpeas for extra protein. Variety keeps your taste buds happy!

recipes.name: Lighter Eggplant Parmesan Stacks
steps: Brush eggplant slices with olive oil and bake or grill until tender and lightly browned.
Layer eggplant with marinara sauce and mozzarella cheese in small stacks.
Bake at 375°F (190°C) for 20-25 minutes until cheese is melted and bubbly. Garnish with fresh basil.
recipes.tip: Baking eggplant instead of frying reduces fat. Healthy doesn't mean boring!

recipes.name: Sweet Potato & Black Bean Tacos
steps: Toss diced sweet potato with chili powder and roast until tender.
Warm black beans. Assemble tacos with warmed corn tortillas, roasted sweet potato, and black beans.
Top with shredded red cabbage, sliced avocado, and a squeeze of fresh lime juice.
recipes.tip: Make your own quick salsa. Fresh ingredients elevate any meal!

recipes.name: Creamy Broccoli Cheddar Soup
steps: Sauté onion and garlic until soft. Stir in flour, then slowly whisk in vegetable broth and milk.
Add broccoli florets and simmer until tender. Use an immersion blender to partially purée.
Stir in shredded cheddar cheese until melted and smooth. Serve warm.
recipes.tip: Use whole wheat flour for fiber. Every small swap counts toward health!

recipes.name: Halloumi & Veggie Skewers
steps: Thread halloumi, zucchini, bell peppers, tomatoes, and red onion onto skewers.
Brush skewers with olive oil and sprinkle with oregano.
Grill or bake at 400°F (200°C) for 15-20 minutes, turning occasionally, until golden and tender.
recipes.tip: Try grilling for smoky flavor. Protein and fiber working for you!

recipes.name: Creamy Tomato Pasta with Spinach
steps: Cook pasta according to package directions. Drain and reserve some pasta water.
Sauté garlic in olive oil, then add crushed tomatoes and simmer for 10 minutes.
Stir in heavy cream and spinach until wilted. Add cooked pasta and a splash of pasta water if needed. Serve with Parmesan.
recipes.tip: Use a plant-based cream for a vegan version. Adapt and thrive!

recipes.name: Mushroom & Asparagus Risotto
steps: Sauté onion and garlic in olive oil. Add rice and stir for 1 minute until translucent.
Add mushrooms and asparagus, then gradually stir in warm broth, one ladleful at a time, until absorbed.
Continue stirring and adding broth for about 20-25 minutes until rice is creamy and al dente. Stir in Parmesan.
recipes.tip: Use plenty of broth to achieve creaminess. Fuel your body with whole grains!

recipes.name: Spicy Peanut Noodles with Edamame
steps: Cook spaghetti according to package directions, adding edamame during the last 3 minutes. Drain.
Whisk together peanut butter, soy sauce, lime juice, ginger, and sriracha for the sauce.
Toss cooked spaghetti and edamame with the sauce and sliced bell pepper until well coated. Serve warm or cold.
recipes.tip: Add chopped peanuts for crunch. Embrace healthy fats for sustained energy!

recipes.name: Protein-Packed Tofu Breakfast Burrito
steps: Crumble tofu into a skillet. Sauté with diced bell pepper until tofu is lightly browned.
Stir in black beans, spinach, and nutritional yeast. Cook until spinach wilts.
Spoon filling into warm tortillas, top with salsa, and roll into burritos.
recipes.tip: Prepare filling ahead for quick morning meals. Fuel your day, the smart way!

recipes.name: Pan-Seared Cauliflower Steaks Chimichurri
steps: Slice cauliflower into 1-inch thick 'steaks'. Brush with olive oil and pan-sear until golden brown and tender.
Combine parsley, cilantro, minced garlic, red wine vinegar, red pepper flakes, and remaining olive oil for chimichurri.
Serve cauliflower steaks immediately, generously topped with fresh chimichurri sauce.
recipes.tip: Marinate cauliflower briefly for deeper flavor. Eat your vibrant greens!

recipes.name: Hearty Vegetable Lasagna Cashew Ricotta
steps: Blend soaked cashews with nutritional yeast, lemon juice, and garlic to create cashew ricotta.
Layer marinara, lasagna noodles, spinach, zucchini slices, and cashew ricotta in a baking dish.
Repeat layers, ending with sauce. Bake at 375°F (190°C) for 45-50 minutes until bubbly and golden.
recipes.tip: Add mushrooms for extra umami. Nutrient-dense meals are delicious!

recipes.name: Lemon Herb Salmon & Asparagus
steps: Preheat oven/air fryer to 400°F (200°C). Toss asparagus with olive oil, salt, pepper.
Season salmon with dill, garlic powder, salt, pepper. Squeeze half lemon over.
Bake salmon and asparagus for 12-15 minutes until salmon is cooked through and flakes.
Serve immediately with remaining lemon wedges.
recipes.tip: Focus on quality protein! Salmon provides Omega-3s essential for a healthy body.

recipes.name: Turkey & Spinach Scramble
steps: Heat olive oil in a non-stick pan. Add ground turkey and cook, breaking it up, until browned.
Add spinach and garlic powder; cook until spinach wilts.
Whisk eggs and egg whites with salt and pepper. Pour into the pan.
Scramble until cooked to your desired consistency. Serve hot.
recipes.tip: Lean protein with veggies is a winning combo! Boost your breakfast with nutrient-rich ingredients.

recipes.name: Black Bean Corn Avocado Salad
steps: Drain and rinse black beans and corn. Dice avocado and red onion. Chop cilantro.
Combine black beans, corn, avocado, red onion, and cilantro in a bowl.
Whisk together lime juice, olive oil, salt, and cumin for the dressing.
Pour dressing over salad and toss gently to combine. Serve immediately.
recipes.tip: Fiber-rich beans and healthy fats fuel your body. Great for a quick, plant-based lunch!

recipes.name: Mediterranean Chickpea Salad
steps: Drain and rinse chickpeas. Dice cucumber, red onion, halve tomatoes, slice olives, chop parsley.
Combine chickpeas, cucumber, tomatoes, red onion, olives, and parsley in a large bowl.
Whisk together lemon juice, olive oil, oregano, salt, and pepper for the dressing.
Pour dressing over the salad and toss gently to combine. Enjoy fresh!
recipes.tip: Plant-based protein power! This vibrant salad keeps you full and nourished with minimal effort.

recipes.name: Spicy Shrimp Zucchini Skillet
steps: Pat shrimp dry. Dice zucchini. Mince garlic.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add zucchini and cook for 3-4 minutes.
Add shrimp, garlic, red pepper flakes, paprika, salt, and pepper. Cook 3-5 minutes until shrimp turn pink.
Serve immediately for a speedy, flavorful meal.
recipes.tip: Quick protein and vibrant veggies make a perfect, low-carb meal. Feel the fiery flavor!

recipes.name: Tuna Salad Lettuce Wraps
steps: Drain tuna thoroughly.
In a bowl, combine tuna, Greek yogurt, diced celery, minced red onion, Dijon mustard, salt, and pepper.
Mix well until combined.
Spoon the tuna salad mixture into individual lettuce cups. Serve immediately.
recipes.tip: Ditch the bread! Lettuce wraps save carbs and boost greens. Lean protein for the win.

recipes.name: Quick Chicken Broccoli Stir-fry
steps: Slice chicken breast into thin strips. Chop broccoli into small florets.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet. Add chicken and cook until browned. Remove from pan.
Add broccoli to skillet, stir-fry 3-5 min. Add garlic and ginger, cook 1 min more.
Return chicken. Add soy sauce and sesame oil. Toss to coat and heat through. Serve hot.
recipes.tip: Load up on fiber and lean protein. This quick stir-fry is a powerhouse for your goals!

recipes.name: Caprese Chicken Skillet
steps: Slice chicken breast horizontally to make thinner cutlets. Season with salt, pepper, garlic powder.
Heat olive oil in a skillet. Cook chicken 4-5 minutes per side until cooked through.
Add halved cherry tomatoes, cook 2 minutes. Place mozzarella over chicken, cover briefly until melted.
Garnish with fresh basil and a drizzle of balsamic glaze. Serve.
recipes.tip: Enjoy rich flavors without the fuss! This easy meal delivers great protein and healthy fats.

recipes.name: Speedy Tofu Scramble Fiesta
steps: Press tofu well, then crumble into a bowl. Add nutritional yeast, turmeric, cumin, salt, pepper; mix.
Heat olive oil in a non-stick pan. Sauté chopped bell pepper and onion for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Add crumbled tofu mixture to the pan. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 5-7 minutes.
Stir in spinach until wilted. Serve hot as a high-protein, plant-based meal.
recipes.tip: Power up your morning the vegan way! Tofu scramble is loaded with protein and quick to prepare.

recipes.name: Smoked Salmon Cucumber Bites
steps: Slice cucumber into 1/2-inch thick rounds.
In a small bowl, mix light cream cheese with chopped fresh dill, a squeeze of lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Spread a small amount of cream cheese mixture onto each cucumber slice.
Top each with a piece of smoked salmon. Serve immediately.
recipes.tip: These elegant bites are bursting with flavor and healthy fats. A perfect low-carb, high-protein snack!

recipes.name: Egg & Avocado Lettuce Wraps
steps: Chop hard-boiled eggs. Mash avocado in a bowl.
Combine chopped eggs, mashed avocado, Greek yogurt, minced red onion, diced celery, Dijon mustard, salt, and pepper.
Mix until well combined, but still chunky.
Spoon the egg salad mixture into fresh lettuce leaves. Enjoy.
recipes.tip: Quick, creamy, and satisfying! Get healthy fats and protein without the extra carbs.

recipes.name: Cottage Cheese Berry Power Bowl
steps: Spoon cottage cheese into a bowl.
Top with mixed berries, slivered almonds, and chia seeds.
Drizzle with honey or sprinkle with Stevia if desired.
Mix gently or enjoy layers. A fast, high-protein start to your day!
recipes.tip: Fuel your body with quality protein and antioxidants! This bowl is a quick win.

recipes.name: Quick Peanut Tofu Stir-fry
steps: Press and dice tofu into 1-inch cubes. Slice bell pepper.
Heat olive oil in a skillet. Add tofu and cook until golden brown. Remove.
Add bell pepper and snap peas, stir-fry 3-4 minutes. Whisk peanut butter, soy sauce, rice vinegar, maple syrup.
Return tofu to skillet, pour sauce over, toss to coat. Heat through and serve with sesame seeds.
recipes.tip: Plant-based protein never tasted so good! A quick, flavorful meal packed with goodness.

recipes.name: Steak & Arugula Quick Salad
steps: Season steak with salt, pepper, garlic powder. Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a skillet.
Sear steak for 3-4 minutes per side for medium-rare. Rest for 5 minutes, then slice thinly.
While steak rests, combine arugula, cherry tomatoes, and red onion in a bowl.
Whisk remaining 0.5 tbsp olive oil and balsamic vinegar. Drizzle dressing over salad. Top with sliced steak and serve.
recipes.tip: Lean steak and fresh greens! This satisfying salad is packed with iron and quick to make.

recipes.name: Spinach Feta Egg White Scramble
steps: Heat olive oil in a non-stick skillet. Add spinach and cook until wilted.
Add halved cherry tomatoes and garlic powder, cook for 2 minutes.
Pour egg whites into the pan. Scramble until set, then stir in crumbled feta cheese.
Season with salt and pepper. Serve immediately.
recipes.tip: High protein, low calories! This quick scramble is ideal for a light, satisfying meal.

recipes.name: Greek Yogurt Chicken Salad
steps: Shred or dice cooked chicken breast.
In a bowl, combine chicken, Greek yogurt, diced celery, halved red grapes, Dijon mustard, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Mix until all ingredients are well combined.
Serve in lettuce cups, bell pepper halves, or simply by itself.
recipes.tip: A creamy, satisfying chicken salad without the mayo! High protein, low fat, and delicious.

recipes.name: Sheet Pan Lemon Herb Chicken & Veggies
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a large bowl, toss chicken and vegetables with olive oil, lemon juice, herbs, and garlic powder until evenly coated.
Spread mixture in a single layer on the prepared baking sheet. Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until chicken is cooked through and vegetables are tender.
recipes.tip: Batch cook for quick, healthy meals all week. Swap veggies for variety!

recipes.name: Hearty Lentil & Roasted Veggie Power Bowl
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Toss diced sweet potato with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper. Roast for 25-30 minutes.
In a large skillet, sauté onion and garlic in 1 tbsp olive oil until softened. Add cooked lentils, cumin, and spinach, cooking until spinach wilts.
Combine roasted sweet potatoes, lentil mixture, and apple cider vinegar in a bowl. Serve warm or cold.
recipes.tip: Prep a big batch of lentils; versatile base for many meals.

recipes.name: Lean Turkey Quinoa Stuffed Bell Peppers
steps: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Lightly grease a baking dish.
In a skillet, brown ground turkey with olive oil and onion. Drain fat. Stir in cooked quinoa, 0.5 cup tomato sauce, and Italian seasoning.
Spoon mixture into bell pepper halves. Place in baking dish and pour remaining tomato sauce around peppers.
Bake for 30-40 minutes, or until peppers are tender and filling is heated through.
recipes.tip: Fill with extra veggies for a fiber boost, or swap ground chicken.

recipes.name: Baked Salmon with Asparagus & Dill
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Arrange salmon fillets and asparagus on the baking sheet. Drizzle with olive oil, sprinkle with garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Top salmon with lemon slices and dill. Bake for 12-15 minutes, or until salmon is cooked through and flakes easily.
recipes.tip: Omega-3 rich for brain health. Roasts quickly for meal prep!

recipes.name: Zesty Tofu Scramble & Bean Bowl
steps: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add onion and bell pepper; cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add crumbled tofu to the skillet. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 8-10 minutes until slightly browned.
Stir in black beans, salsa, nutritional yeast, salt, and pepper. Cook for 2-3 minutes, then add spinach until wilted.
Serve hot in bowls. Great for breakfast or lunch prep.
recipes.tip: Press tofu well for best texture. Great for breakfast or lunch!

recipes.name: Mediterranean Chicken Salad Lettuce Wraps
steps: In a large bowl, combine shredded chicken, Greek yogurt, chopped celery, and red onion.
Stir in lemon juice, Dijon mustard, salt, and pepper. Mix until well combined.
Serve spoonfuls of chicken salad in fresh lettuce cups. Store remaining salad refrigerated.
recipes.tip: Use rotisserie chicken for a super fast, lean prep meal.

recipes.name: Spinach Feta Veggie Egg Muffins
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Lightly spray a 12-cup muffin tin with olive oil.
In a large bowl, whisk eggs with almond milk, salt, and pepper. Stir in chopped spinach, feta, and bell pepper.
Pour egg mixture evenly into the muffin cups, filling about two-thirds full.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until set and lightly golden. Let cool before storing.
recipes.tip: Freeze individual muffins for quick protein-packed breakfasts.

recipes.name: Lean Beef & Broccoli Stir-Fry
steps: Whisk together soy sauce, ginger, garlic, sesame oil, and cornstarch in a small bowl. Set aside.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add beef and stir-fry until browned, about 3-4 minutes. Remove beef and set aside.
Add broccoli florets to the skillet and stir-fry for 5-7 minutes until tender-crisp. Return beef to the skillet.
Pour sauce over beef and broccoli, tossing to coat. Cook for 1-2 minutes until sauce thickens. Serve immediately.
recipes.tip: Serve with cauliflower rice for extra low-carb goodness.

recipes.name: Creamy Chickpea Sweet Potato Curry
steps: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Sauté onion, garlic, and ginger until fragrant, about 5 minutes.
Stir in curry powder and cook for 1 minute. Add diced sweet potato, chickpeas, and coconut milk.
Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook for 20-25 minutes, or until sweet potatoes are tender.
Stir in fresh spinach until wilted. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Serve warm.
recipes.tip: This curry freezes beautifully; make a large batch for convenience.

recipes.name: Salsa Verde Shredded Chicken Bowls
steps: Place chicken breasts in a large pot or slow cooker. Add chopped onion, minced garlic, salsa verde, and chicken broth.
Bring to a simmer on the stovetop (or cook on high for 3-4 hours in slow cooker). Cover and cook until chicken is easily shredded, about 25-30 minutes.
Remove chicken and shred using two forks. Return shredded chicken to the pot and mix with the sauce.
Stir in fresh cilantro. Serve in bowls, topped with diced avocado.
recipes.tip: Shredded chicken is versatile: tacos, salads, or mixed with rice.

recipes.name: Vibrant Mediterranean Quinoa Salad
steps: In a large bowl, combine cooked quinoa, diced cucumber, halved cherry tomatoes, chopped red onion, and sliced Kalamata olives.
In a small bowl, whisk together lemon juice, olive oil, salt, and pepper to make the dressing.
Pour dressing over the quinoa mixture and toss to combine. Gently fold in crumbled feta and fresh parsley. Serve chilled.
recipes.tip: Add grilled chicken or chickpeas for extra protein power.

recipes.name: Shrimp Scampi Zucchini Noodle Delight
steps: Heat olive oil and butter in a large skillet over medium heat. Add garlic and red pepper flakes; cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add shrimp to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes per side, until pink and cooked through. Remove shrimp from skillet and set aside.
Add zucchini noodles to the skillet. Toss and cook for 2-3 minutes until slightly softened but still crisp. Return shrimp to skillet, add lemon juice and parsley, toss to combine. Serve immediately.
recipes.tip: Swap zucchini for spaghetti squash for another low-carb noodle option.

recipes.name: Peanut Tempeh & Broccoli Bowl
steps: In a bowl, whisk together peanut butter, soy sauce, lime juice, ginger, garlic, and 1 tbsp water to create the sauce.
Heat sesame oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add cubed tempeh and cook until golden brown, about 5-7 minutes. Remove and set aside.
Add broccoli florets to the skillet and stir-fry for 5-7 minutes until tender-crisp. Return tempeh to the skillet.
Pour peanut sauce over tempeh and broccoli, tossing to coat. Cook for 1-2 minutes until heated through and sauce thickens slightly.
recipes.tip: Tempeh is a protein powerhouse; great for plant-based meal prep.

recipes.name: Hearty White Bean & Kale Soup
steps: Heat olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Sauté onion and garlic until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add vegetable broth, diced tomatoes, cannellini beans, and Italian seasoning. Bring to a simmer.
Add chopped kale to the pot, stirring until it wilts into the soup. Cook for 15-20 minutes to allow flavors to meld. Season to taste.
recipes.tip: Soup is perfect for cold weather meal prep. Freezes well!

recipes.name: Baked Cod with Root Vegetables
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a large baking sheet with parchment paper.
Toss chopped carrots and parsnips with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper. Spread on one half of the baking sheet and roast for 15 minutes.

Remove sheet from oven. Place cod fillets on the other half. Drizzle cod with remaining 1 tbsp olive oil, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Top with lemon slices and thyme sprigs. Bake for another 12-15 minutes, or until cod is opaque and flakes easily.
recipes.tip: Cod is lean and mild. Season with your favorite herbs for variety.

recipes.name: Sizzling Chicken Fajita Power Bowl
steps: Heat olive oil in a large skillet or cast iron pan over medium-high heat. Add sliced chicken and cook until browned, about 5-7 minutes. Remove chicken and set aside.
Add sliced bell peppers and red onion to the skillet. Stir-fry for 7-10 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp.
Return chicken to the skillet. Sprinkle with fajita seasoning and cook for another 2-3 minutes, tossing to coat and heat through.
Remove from heat, stir in lime juice and fresh cilantro. Serve hot in bowls.
recipes.tip: Top with fresh salsa or guac for healthy fats and flavor.

recipes.name: Spicy Peanut Noodle Meal Prep
steps: Cook spaghetti according to package directions. Drain and set aside. Air fry or pan-fry cubed tofu until golden and crispy.
Steam or lightly sauté broccoli and red bell pepper until tender-crisp.
In a large bowl, whisk together peanut butter, soy sauce, rice vinegar, sriracha, and 2-3 tablespoons of warm water until smooth.
Add cooked spaghetti, tofu, broccoli, and bell pepper to the bowl with the sauce. Toss to coat evenly. Serve warm or chilled.
recipes.tip: Add extra veggies like carrots or snow peas for more crunch and nutrients.

recipes.name: Buffalo Chicken Meal Prep Bowls
steps: In a bowl, combine shredded chicken with buffalo hot sauce until thoroughly coated.
Divide romaine lettuce among four meal prep containers. Top with buffalo chicken, celery sticks, and carrot sticks.
Drizzle with Greek yogurt ranch dressing and sprinkle with blue cheese crumbles if desired. Store in the refrigerator.
Meal prep tip: keep dressing separate until serving for optimal freshness.
recipes.tip: Control spice levels by adjusting the buffalo sauce amount. Add a protein boost!

recipes.name: Ground Beef & Cauliflower Rice Skillet
steps: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add ground beef and onion; cook, breaking up beef, until browned. Drain excess fat.
Stir in minced garlic, soy sauce, and sriracha (if using). Cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add cauliflower rice to the skillet. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 5-7 minutes, until cauliflower is tender and heated through.
Season with salt and pepper to taste. Garnish with sliced scallions before serving. Divide into meal prep containers.
recipes.tip: A quick, satisfying meal prep. Adjust spice to your preference!

recipes.name: Roasted Vegetable & Halloumi Bake
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a large baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a large bowl, toss zucchini, cherry tomatoes, red onion, and bell pepper with olive oil, oregano, salt, and pepper.
Spread vegetables in a single layer on the baking sheet. Roast for 20 minutes. Add sliced halloumi to the baking sheet.
Continue roasting for another 10-15 minutes, or until vegetables are tender and halloumi is golden brown. Garnish with fresh parsley.
recipes.tip: High-protein halloumi makes this veggie bake a satisfying meal.

recipes.name: Spicy Turkey Sausage Egg Scramble
steps: Heat olive oil in a non-stick pan over medium heat. Add turkey sausage and bell pepper, cook until sausage is browned and bell pepper is tender.
Whisk eggs with sriracha. Pour into the pan. Stir gently as eggs begin to set.
Add spinach, cook until wilted. Serve immediately.
recipes.tip: Swap bell peppers for mushrooms. Boost your metabolism with this protein-packed start!

recipes.name: Greek Yogurt Berry Nut Bowl
steps: Spoon Greek yogurt into a bowl. Stir in stevia if desired.
Top with mixed berries, chopped walnuts, and chia seeds.
Enjoy immediately for a quick, balanced breakfast.
recipes.tip: Add a scoop of protein powder for an extra boost. Fuel your body right!

recipes.name: Hearty Tofu Scramble with Veggies
steps: Crumble pressed tofu into a bowl. Mix with nutritional yeast, turmeric, and black salt.
Heat olive oil in a non-stick pan. Sauté onion and bell pepper until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add crumbled tofu mixture and cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until slightly browned.
Stir in spinach and cook until wilted. Serve hot.
recipes.tip: Add black beans for extra fiber. Plant-based power to kickstart your day!

recipes.name: Smoked Salmon Cream Cheese Cucumbers
steps: In a small bowl, mix light cream cheese with chopped fresh dill and black pepper.
Spread a small amount of the dill cream cheese onto each cucumber slice.
Top each slice with a piece of smoked salmon and a few capers. Arrange on a platter and serve.
recipes.tip: Use everything bagel seasoning for extra flavor. Keep it light, make it delicious!

recipes.name: Green Power Protein Smoothie
steps: Combine almond milk, protein powder, spinach, chia seeds, almond butter, and frozen berries in a blender.
Blend until completely smooth and creamy. Add a little more almond milk if too thick.
Pour into a glass and enjoy immediately.
recipes.tip: Add ice for a colder, thicker shake. Conquer your morning with this nutrient-dense fuel!

recipes.name: Cottage Cheese Tomato Bowl
steps: Spoon cottage cheese into a bowl.
Arrange halved cherry tomatoes and diced avocado on top of the cottage cheese.
Sprinkle generously with everything bagel seasoning and chopped fresh chives. Serve immediately.
recipes.tip: Add a dash of hot sauce for a kick. Simple, satisfying, and packed with protein!

recipes.name: Savory Beef Bell Pepper Hash
steps: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add ground beef, breaking it apart, and cook until browned. Drain excess fat.
Add diced bell peppers, onion, and minced garlic to the skillet. Season with paprika, salt, and pepper.
Sauté for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until vegetables are tender and slightly caramelized. Serve hot.
recipes.tip: Top with a fried egg for extra protein. Stay strong and fueled all morning!

recipes.name: Spinach Feta Egg Muffins
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Lightly spray a 6-cup muffin tin with olive oil.
In a bowl, whisk eggs with milk, salt, and pepper. Stir in chopped spinach and crumbled feta cheese.
Pour egg mixture evenly into the muffin cups. Bake for 18-22 minutes, or until set and lightly golden. Cool slightly before serving.
recipes.tip: Make ahead for quick meal prep. Grab and go, no excuses!

recipes.name: Chickpea Avocado Lettuce Wraps
steps: In a bowl, mash chickpeas with avocado. Stir in celery, red onion, and lemon juice.
Season with salt and pepper to taste. Mix well to combine.
Spoon the chickpea mixture into large lettuce leaves. Fold or roll up like tacos. Serve fresh.
recipes.tip: Add a sprinkle of everything bagel seasoning. Perfect plant-based protein for a vibrant morning!

recipes.name: Steak Bell Pepper Egg Scramble
steps: Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add steak, season with salt, pepper, and chili powder. Cook until browned, remove from pan.
Add bell pepper and onion to the same skillet, cook until softened, about 5-7 minutes.
Whisk eggs and pour into the skillet with veggies. Scramble until cooked through. Return steak to pan and toss to combine. Serve hot.
recipes.tip: Serve with a side of salsa for extra flavor. Get powerful protein to dominate your day!

recipes.name: Sardine Avocado Cucumber Boats

ingredients.name: canned sardines in olive oil, drained

steps: Halve cucumber lengthwise and scoop out seeds to create 'boats'.
In a bowl, flake sardines. Add diced avocado, lemon juice, chopped parsley, and Dijon mustard. Mash gently and mix.
Fill cucumber boats generously with the sardine mixture. Serve immediately.
recipes.tip: Add a sprinkle of red pepper flakes. Omega-3 rich fuel for brain and body!

recipes.name: Ricotta Herb Scrambled Eggs
steps: Whisk eggs in a bowl with salt and pepper until well combined.
Heat butter or olive oil in a non-stick skillet over medium-low heat. Pour in eggs.
As eggs begin to set, gently fold in ricotta cheese, chives, and parsley. Continue cooking until desired consistency. Serve immediately.
recipes.tip: Add a pinch of red pepper flakes. Creamy, satisfying, and packed with muscle-building protein!

recipes.name: Savory Lentil Spinach Bowl
steps: In a bowl, whisk tahini with lemon juice, olive oil, and cumin until smooth. Add a splash of water if too thick.
In a separate pan, gently warm cooked lentils with spinach and cherry tomatoes until spinach wilts.
Transfer lentil mixture to a bowl, top with finely diced red onion, and drizzle generously with tahini dressing. Serve warm.
recipes.tip: Add a sprinkle of toasted seeds for crunch. Fiber and protein for lasting energy!

recipes.name: Chicken Apple Sausage Kale Skillet
steps: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add diced sweet potato and onion, cook until softened (8-10 mins).
Slice chicken apple sausage and add to the skillet. Cook for 3-5 minutes until lightly browned.
Stir in chopped kale, garlic powder, and red pepper flakes. Cook until kale is tender-crisp. Serve hot.
recipes.tip: Top with a poached egg for extra richness. Wholesome and satisfying to crush your goals!

recipes.name: Peanut Butter Chia Seed Pudding
steps: In a jar or container, whisk together almond milk, chia seeds, peanut butter, protein powder, and maple syrup/stevia.
Stir well to combine and break up any chia seed clumps. Let sit for 5 minutes, then stir again.
Refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or overnight, until thickened. Top with mixed berries before serving.
recipes.tip: Add cocoa powder for a chocolatey twist. Prep tonight, crush tomorrow!

recipes.name: Avocado & Egg Bagel Spice Bowl
steps: Peel and halve hard-boiled eggs. Arrange in a bowl.
Add diced avocado and halved cherry tomatoes to the bowl.
Generously sprinkle with everything bagel seasoning and chopped chives. Serve.
recipes.tip: Add a drizzle of hot sauce for a kick. Quick, easy, and satisfying protein powerhouse!

recipes.name: Cashew Chicken Broccoli Stir-fry
steps: Slice chicken, stir-fry in a hot pan with sesame oil until cooked through.
Add broccoli and bell pepper, cook for 3-5 minutes until tender-crisp.
Stir in soy sauce, ginger, and garlic, tossing to coat.
Remove from heat, mix in cashews, and serve immediately.
recipes.tip: Boost fiber and micronutrients by adding extra colorful veggies!

recipes.name: Miso Glazed Salmon with Asparagus
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Whisk miso, mirin, soy sauce, ginger, and sesame oil for glaze.
Place salmon on a baking sheet, brush with half the glaze. Toss asparagus with a drizzle of oil.
Roast salmon and asparagus for 12-15 minutes until salmon is cooked and asparagus is tender.
Brush remaining glaze on salmon before serving.
recipes.tip: For extra greens, add steamed edamame or a side salad.

recipes.name: Spicy Peanut Tofu Noodle Bowl
steps: Cube tofu and pan-fry until golden. Cook rice noodles according to package directions.
Whisk together peanut butter, soy sauce, lime juice, chili garlic sauce, and a splash of water for the sauce.
Sauté bell pepper and carrots for 3-4 minutes. Add spinach and cook until wilted.
Combine cooked tofu, noodles, veggies, and sauce in a large bowl, tossing to coat.
recipes.tip: Add thinly sliced cucumbers for a refreshing crunch and cooling effect.

recipes.name: Garlic Ginger Beef Broccoli Stir-fry
steps: Slice beef thinly against the grain. Whisk soy sauce, ginger, garlic, and cornstarch for marinade.
Marinate beef for 10 minutes. Heat sesame oil in a wok or large skillet over high heat.
Stir-fry beef until browned, remove from pan. Add broccoli, cook until tender-crisp.
Return beef to pan, toss everything together for 1 minute and serve.
recipes.tip: Serve over cauliflower rice to keep carbs super low.

recipes.name: Kimchi Shrimp Fried Rice
steps: Heat oil in a large skillet. Sauté shrimp until pink, remove from pan.
Add garlic and kimchi to the pan, cook for 2 minutes. Stir in cooked rice.
Push rice to one side, scramble egg on the other. Mix egg into rice.
Return shrimp, add soy sauce and sesame oil. Stir well and garnish with green onions.
recipes.tip: Use day-old rice for the best fried rice texture and flavor.

recipes.name: Green Curry Tofu Chickpea Bowl
steps: Sauté onion and bell pepper in a pot until soft. Add curry paste and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in coconut milk, chickpeas, and tofu. Bring to a simmer.
Add spinach and cook until wilted. Stir in lime juice.
Serve hot, optionally over cauliflower rice.
recipes.tip: Serve with cauliflower rice to reduce carbs and boost veggies.

recipes.name: Sheet Pan Teriyaki Chicken Veggies
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Cut chicken into 1-inch pieces.
On a large sheet pan, toss chicken and all vegetables with teriyaki sauce and sesame oil.
Spread ingredients in a single layer.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, stirring halfway, until chicken is cooked through and vegetables are tender.
recipes.tip: Meal prep by making a double batch for lunches all week!

recipes.name: Fresh Tuna Poke Bowl
steps: In a bowl, gently toss tuna cubes with soy sauce, rice vinegar, and sesame oil.
Prepare the cooked brown rice, edamame, cucumber, and avocado.
Assemble the bowl: start with brown rice, top with the marinated tuna.
Arrange edamame, cucumber, and avocado around the tuna. Serve chilled.
recipes.tip: Load up on extra greens like spinach or kale for added nutrients.

recipes.name: Gochujang Tofu Green Bean Stir-fry
steps: Pan-fry cubed tofu in sesame oil until golden and crisp, remove from pan.
Add green beans to the same pan, stir-fry for 5-7 minutes until tender-crisp.
Whisk gochujang, soy sauce, garlic, and ginger with a splash of water for the sauce.
Return tofu to the pan, add sauce, and toss until everything is well coated and heated through.
recipes.tip: Add shiitake mushrooms for an umami boost and extra fiber.

recipes.name: Lemongrass Pork Skewers Salad
steps: Cut pork into 1-inch cubes. Marinate with lemongrass, garlic, fish sauce, soy sauce, and half the lime juice for 15 minutes.
Thread pork onto skewers. Grill or broil until cooked through and slightly charred.
Combine sliced cucumber and red onion with remaining lime juice and a pinch of salt for the salad.
Serve hot pork skewers alongside the refreshing cucumber salad.
recipes.tip: Pair with a side of steamed bok choy for a complete meal.

recipes.name: Steamed Cod Ginger Scallion
steps: Steam cod fillets and bok choy until cod is flaky and bok choy is tender-crisp.
Place steamed cod on a serving plate, top with julienned ginger and half the scallions.
Heat sesame oil in a small pan until shimmering. Carefully pour hot oil over the ginger and scallions.
Drizzle with soy sauce and rice vinegar, then garnish with remaining scallions and serve with bok choy.
recipes.tip: This cooking method preserves nutrients and yields super tender fish.

recipes.name: Tempeh Edamame Lettuce Wraps
steps: Sauté crumbled tempeh in a pan until lightly browned. Add edamame, carrots, and bell pepper, cook 3-4 minutes.
Whisk together soy sauce, peanut butter, lime juice, and ginger with a splash of water for the sauce.
Pour sauce over tempeh-edamame mixture and toss to coat, cook until heated through.
Spoon mixture into fresh lettuce cups and serve immediately.
recipes.tip: Add water chestnuts for extra crunch and texture variety.

recipes.name: Lean Chicken Pho Soup
steps: Combine broth, ginger, star anise, and cinnamon stick in a pot. Add chicken breast, bring to a simmer.
Cook chicken until tender, then remove and shred. Discard spices from broth.
Stir fish sauce into the broth. Cook a small portion of thin rice noodles (optional, for low carb).
Assemble bowls with shredded chicken, bean sprouts, cilantro, lime wedges, and hot broth.
recipes.tip: Load up on fresh herbs like cilantro and mint for vibrant flavor.

recipes.name: Spicy Peanut Tempeh Stir-fry
steps: Sauté crumbled tempeh in a large skillet or wok until lightly browned and fragrant.
Add sliced bell peppers and snow peas, stir-fry for 4-5 minutes until tender-crisp.
Whisk together peanut butter, soy sauce, rice vinegar, sriracha, and ginger with a splash of water for the sauce.
Pour the sauce over the tempeh and vegetables, toss well until everything is coated and heated through. Serve.
recipes.tip: Try adding a squeeze of fresh lime juice for bright acidity.

recipes.name: Korean Beef Bibimbap Bowl
steps: Cook ground beef until browned, drain fat. Season beef with 1 tbsp soy sauce and 1 tsp sesame oil.
Quickly sauté spinach, carrots, and zucchini separately until tender-crisp. Fry the egg sunny-side up.
Assemble bowls with cooked brown rice, topped with sections of beef and sautéed vegetables.
Place the fried egg in the center. Serve with gochujang and remaining soy sauce/sesame oil.
recipes.tip: The variety of textures and flavors makes this dish satisfying and fun.

recipes.name: Chicken Egg Drop Soup
steps: Bring chicken broth to a gentle simmer in a pot. Whisk cornstarch with 2 tbsp cold water to form a slurry.
Stir slurry into simmering broth until slightly thickened. Add shredded chicken, soy sauce, and ginger.
Slowly pour beaten eggs into the simmering broth in a thin stream, stirring gently to create ribbons.
Remove from heat, stir in sesame oil, and garnish with sliced green onions before serving.
recipes.tip: This comforting soup is perfect for a light meal or appetizer.

recipes.name: Spicy Coconut Shrimp Curry
steps: Heat a pot over medium heat, add red curry paste and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in coconut milk and fish sauce, bring to a gentle simmer. Add sliced bell pepper, cook 3-5 minutes.
Add shrimp and cook for 3-5 minutes until pink and cooked through.
Remove from heat, stir in lime juice and spinach until wilted. Garnish with fresh cilantro.
recipes.tip: Serve with cauliflower rice to soak up all the delicious curry sauce.

recipes.name: Korean Gochujang Chicken Bowls
steps: Whisk gochujang, soy sauce, rice vinegar, and garlic for the sauce. Toss cubed chicken with half the sauce.
Sauté chicken in a pan until cooked through. Steam or roast broccoli until tender-crisp.
Assemble bowls with cooked quinoa, chicken, and broccoli.
Top with kimchi, a drizzle of remaining sauce, and sliced green onions.
recipes.tip: For an extra kick, add a pinch of red pepper flakes to your chicken.

recipes.name: Hearty Lentil Edamame Stir-fry
steps: Heat a large skillet or wok with a drizzle of oil. Add carrots, snow peas, and cook for 5 minutes.
Stir in cooked lentils and edamame, cooking for another 3-4 minutes.
Whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, ginger, and garlic for the stir-fry sauce.
Pour sauce over the lentil and vegetable mixture, toss to coat. Garnish with sesame seeds before serving.
recipes.tip: This satisfying plant-based meal is packed with fiber and protein!

recipes.name: Lemon Herb Roasted Chicken Asparagus
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
Toss chicken and asparagus with olive oil, lemon juice, oregano, and garlic powder.
Spread on a baking sheet and roast for 20-25 minutes, until chicken is cooked through.
recipes.tip: Swap chicken for cod for a pescatarian delight! Aim for lean protein daily.

recipes.name: Baked Salmon Dill Roasted Broccoli
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
Toss broccoli with olive oil, salt, and pepper; spread on a baking sheet.
Place salmon on the same sheet, season with salt, pepper, and dill. Bake 15-20 minutes.
recipes.tip: Omega-3s are key for brain health! Roast veggies to boost nutrient intake.

recipes.name: Garlic Butter Steak Bites Green Beans
steps: Cut steak into 1-inch bites. Season with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high. Sear steak bites until browned; set aside.
Add butter and garlic to skillet, sauté green beans until tender-crisp. Return steak and toss.
recipes.tip: Focus on quality protein for satiety and muscle repair. Go for grass-fed options!

recipes.name: Turkey Zucchini Meatballs Marinara
steps: Combine turkey, zucchini, egg, basil, onion powder, salt, and pepper. Form into meatballs.
Heat olive oil in a skillet. Brown meatballs on all sides, then drain excess fat.
Pour marinara sauce over meatballs, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes until cooked through.
recipes.tip: Zucchini adds moisture and nutrients. Prioritize lean protein sources for your goals!

recipes.name: Spiced Tofu Broccoli Stir-fry
steps: Press tofu, then cut into cubes. Toss with 1 tbsp tamari, set aside.
Heat sesame oil in a large skillet. Add tofu and cook until golden brown; remove.
Add broccoli, garlic, ginger to skillet, stir-fry until tender-crisp. Return tofu, add remaining tamari and toss.
recipes.tip: Tofu is a protein powerhouse! Press it well for best texture and flavor absorption.

recipes.name: Spicy Shrimp Scampi Zucchini Noodles
steps: Spiralize zucchini into noodles. Season shrimp with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet. Add garlic and red pepper flakes; sauté briefly.
Add shrimp and cook until pink. Add zucchini noodles and parsley; toss quickly until heated through.
recipes.tip: Zucchini noodles keep it light and low-carb. Shrimp cooks fast, don't overcook!

recipes.name: Chicken Stir-fry Cabbage Bell Peppers
steps: Slice chicken breast into thin strips. Season with salt and pepper.
Heat sesame oil in a large wok or skillet. Stir-fry chicken until cooked through; remove.
Add cabbage, bell pepper, onion, and garlic to wok. Stir-fry until tender-crisp. Return chicken, add coconut aminos and toss.
recipes.tip: Cabbage is a fantastic low-carb base. Load up on colorful veggies for micronutrients!

recipes.name: Cod Olives Tomatoes Herbs
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
Place cod on a baking sheet. Arrange tomatoes and olives around it. Drizzle with olive oil.
Season with oregano, salt, and pepper. Bake for 15-18 minutes until cod is flaky. Garnish with parsley.
recipes.tip: Cod is a lean protein source. Healthy fats from olives boost flavor and satiety!

recipes.name: Spinach Feta Stuffed Chicken Breast
steps: Slice a pocket into the side of the chicken breast. Mix spinach, feta, and garlic powder.
Stuff the spinach-feta mixture into the chicken pocket. Season outside with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in an oven-safe skillet. Sear chicken on both sides, then bake at 375°F (190°C) for 20-25 minutes.
recipes.tip: Stuffing adds flavor and nutrients without extra carbs. Cook once, eat twice!

recipes.name: Hearty Beef Spinach Soup
steps: Brown ground beef in a pot; drain fat. Add onion, celery, carrot; sauté until softened.
Pour in beef broth and diced tomatoes. Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook for 20 minutes.
Stir in fresh spinach until wilted. Season with salt and pepper.
recipes.tip: Soup is a great way to pack in veggies. Make a big batch for easy meal prep.

recipes.name: Tofu Veggie Scramble Nutritional Yeast
steps: Crumble tofu with your hands into a bowl. Stir in nutritional yeast, turmeric, salt, and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a skillet. Sauté bell pepper and onion until tender.
Add crumbled tofu mixture and spinach to the skillet. Cook, stirring, until spinach wilts and tofu is heated through.
recipes.tip: Nutritional yeast adds a cheesy flavor. Start your day with plant-based power!

recipes.name: Sheet Pan Sausage Peppers
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Slice sausage into rounds.
On a large baking sheet, toss sausage, bell peppers, and onion with olive oil, paprika, and garlic powder.
Roast for 20-25 minutes, stirring halfway, until veggies are tender and sausage is browned.
recipes.tip: Sheet pan meals mean minimal cleanup! Check labels for sugar-free sausage.

recipes.name: Hearty Tempeh Kale Skillet
steps: Crumble tempeh into a skillet with olive oil. Cook until browned and slightly crispy.
Add red onion and garlic; sauté until softened. Stir in kale and cook until wilted.
Add tamari and a splash of water to deglaze. Drizzle with tahini before serving.
recipes.tip: Tempeh is a fermented soy powerhouse! It's great for gut health and protein.

recipes.name: Halloumi Roasted Vegetable Skewers
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Soak skewers in water if wooden.
Cut halloumi into cubes. Toss halloumi and vegetables with olive oil and dried herbs.
Thread halloumi and veggies onto skewers. Roast for 20-25 minutes, turning occasionally, until golden.
recipes.tip: Halloumi offers satisfying vegetarian protein. Mind your portion sizes for best results!

recipes.name: Pork Tenderloin Sautéed Cabbage
steps: Slice pork tenderloin into medallions. Season with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a skillet. Sear pork medallions 3-4 minutes per side; remove.
Add cabbage, garlic to skillet, sauté until tender. Stir in apple cider vinegar and Dijon. Return pork to heat through.
recipes.tip: Pork tenderloin is lean and delicious. Cabbage provides filling fiber and crunch.

recipes.name: Deconstructed Cabbage Roll Skillet
steps: Heat olive oil in a large skillet. Brown ground beef and onion; drain fat.
Add garlic, shredded cabbage, diced tomatoes, and paprika. Season with salt and pepper.
Cover and simmer for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until cabbage is tender.
