recipes.tip: All the comfort of cabbage rolls, less fuss! Boost fiber with extra cabbage.

recipes.name: Greek Yogurt Protein Power Bowl
steps: Spoon Greek yogurt into a bowl.
Top with mixed berries, sliced almonds, and chia seeds.
Drizzle with honey and enjoy immediately.
recipes.tip: Boost protein with more seeds or a scoop of powder!

recipes.name: Turkey & Cheese Deli Roll-Ups
steps: Lay out turkey slices on a clean surface.
Place cheese slices and spinach evenly over the turkey.
Spread Dijon mustard lightly on the spinach.
Roll up tightly and slice into desired portions.
recipes.tip: Swap cheddar for provolone to vary flavors easily.

recipes.name: Spicy Steamed Edamame Pods
steps: Steam edamame pods according to package directions until tender-crisp.
Drain well and transfer to a bowl.
Toss with olive oil, sea salt, and chili flakes until evenly coated.
recipes.tip: A pinch of garlic powder adds extra savory zest.

recipes.name: Cottage Cheese Cucumber Bites
steps: Slice cucumber into thick rounds.
Top each cucumber slice with a dollop of cottage cheese.
Garnish with fresh dill, a grind of black pepper, and halved cherry tomatoes.
recipes.tip: Add a dash of everything bagel seasoning for crunch.

recipes.name: Everything Bagel Hard-Boiled Eggs
steps: Place eggs in a saucepan, cover with water, bring to boil.
Boil for 8-10 minutes, then transfer to an ice bath for 5 minutes.
Peel the cooled eggs, slice in half, and sprinkle generously with seasoning.
recipes.tip: Prep a batch of eggs for grab-and-go convenience!

recipes.name: Apple Slices with Almond Butter
steps: Core and slice the apple into thin wedges.
Spread almond butter on each apple slice.
Dust lightly with cinnamon for extra flavor.
recipes.tip: Use any nut butter; check for no added sugars.

recipes.name: Smoked Salmon Cucumber Bites
steps: Slice cucumber into thick rounds.
Spread a small amount of cream cheese on each cucumber slice.
Top with a piece of smoked salmon and garnish with dill and lemon zest.
recipes.tip: Swap cream cheese for avocado for a dairy-free option!

recipes.name: Spicy Roasted Chickpeas
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Drain, rinse, and thoroughly dry chickpeas.
Toss chickpeas with olive oil and all spices on a baking sheet.
Roast for 20-25 minutes, stirring halfway, until crispy and golden.
recipes.tip: Batch cook these for quick, crunchy snack access!

recipes.name: Bell Pepper Strips with Hummus
steps: Wash and core bell peppers, then slice into thin strips.
Arrange pepper strips on a plate.
Serve with a generous dollop of hummus and a sprinkle of black pepper.
recipes.tip: Choose colorful peppers for extra visual appeal and nutrients.

recipes.name: Green Protein Power Smoothie
steps: Combine spinach, banana, almond milk, protein powder, and chia seeds in a blender.
Blend on high until completely smooth and creamy.
Pour into a glass and enjoy immediately.
recipes.tip: Add ice for a colder, thicker, more refreshing drink!

recipes.name: High-Fiber Avocado Toast Bites
steps: Toast bread until golden and crisp.
Mash avocado with lemon juice.
Spread mashed avocado evenly on the toast.
Sprinkle with everything bagel seasoning and red pepper flakes, then slice into 'bites'.
recipes.tip: Choose dense whole grain bread for more filling fiber.

recipes.name: Zesty Shrimp Cocktail
steps: In a small bowl, whisk together ketchup, lemon juice, horseradish, and hot sauce.
Arrange chilled cooked shrimp on a plate with celery sticks.
Serve with the zesty homemade cocktail sauce for dipping.
recipes.tip: Make your own sauce to control sugar and sodium intake.

recipes.name: Black Bean Mini Pepper Nachos
steps: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Halve and deseed mini peppers.
Fill pepper halves with black beans, salsa, and nutritional yeast. Arrange on a baking sheet.
Bake for 10-12 minutes until peppers are slightly tender and warm.
Top with diced avocado and fresh cilantro before serving.
recipes.tip: Use nutritional yeast for a cheesy flavor without dairy.

recipes.name: Ricotta & Cherry Tomato Toasts
steps: Toast bread until golden.
Spread ricotta cheese evenly over each slice.
Top with halved cherry tomatoes and fresh basil leaves.
Drizzle with olive oil and season with black pepper.
recipes.tip: A pinch of red pepper flakes adds a nice spicy kick.

recipes.name: Tuna Salad Celery Boats
steps: Drain tuna well. In a bowl, mix tuna with mayonnaise, Dijon mustard, lemon juice, and pepper.
Wash and trim celery stalks. Fill each stalk with a portion of the tuna mixture.
Serve immediately or chill for later. Enjoy this crunchy, protein-packed snack!
recipes.tip: Add chopped pickles for extra crunch and tang.

recipes.name: Spiced Nut & Seed Mix
steps: Preheat oven to 300°F (150°C).
In a bowl, toss nuts and seeds with olive oil and all spices until evenly coated.
Spread mixture in a single layer on a baking sheet.
Roast for 15-18 minutes, stirring halfway, until fragrant and lightly toasted.
recipes.tip: Portion into small bags for easy, pre-measured snacks.

recipes.name: Spiced Chicken Tikka Skewers
steps: Marinate chicken in yogurt, lemon, ginger-garlic paste, and garam masala for 15 mins.
Skewer chicken and vegetables, alternating pieces.
Grill or bake skewers at 400°F (200°C) for 15-20 minutes, until chicken is cooked through.
recipes.tip: Grill extra chicken for healthy meal prep bowls.

recipes.name: Hearty Spinach Red Lentil Dal
steps: Sauté chopped onion, garlic, ginger, and cumin seeds in a pot.
Add lentils, diced tomato, turmeric, and vegetable broth; bring to a boil then simmer 20 mins.
Stir in fresh spinach until wilted, serve hot.
recipes.tip: Serve with brown rice for a complete protein meal.

recipes.name: Quick Chickpea & Veggie Curry
steps: Sauté chopped onion, garlic, and ginger until fragrant.
Add curry powder, chickpeas, mixed vegetables, diced tomatoes, and coconut milk.
Simmer for 15-20 minutes until vegetables are tender, stir in fresh cilantro.
recipes.tip: Bulk up with more veggies for extra fiber boost.

recipes.name: Tandoori Salmon with Quinoa
steps: Marinate salmon in yogurt, tandoori masala, and a squeeze of lemon juice for 15 minutes.
Bake or grill salmon at 400°F (200°C) for 12-15 minutes, until flaky.
Serve salmon over cooked quinoa with diced cucumber, tomato, and fresh mint.
recipes.tip: Add a drizzle of lime juice for a zingy finish.

recipes.name: Healthier Paneer Butter Masala
steps: Sauté chopped onion until soft. Add ginger-garlic paste, garam masala, and turmeric.
Blend crushed tomatoes and soaked cashews until smooth; add to the pot and simmer 10 mins.
Add paneer cubes and light cream, simmer gently for 5-7 minutes. Stir in fenugreek leaves.
recipes.tip: Serve with cauliflower rice to keep carbs low.

recipes.name: Lean Ground Turkey Keema Curry
steps: Brown ground turkey in a pan, breaking it up. Drain any fat.
Add chopped onion, ginger-garlic paste, garam masala, cumin, and turmeric; sauté until fragrant.
Stir in diced tomato and frozen peas. Simmer for 10-15 minutes until cooked through. Garnish with cilantro.
recipes.tip: Enjoy in lettuce wraps for an extra low-carb meal.

recipes.name: Classic Aloo Gobi (Cauliflower Potato)
steps: Sauté chopped onion, garlic, ginger, green chilies, cumin seeds, turmeric, and coriander powder.
Add diced potatoes and cauliflower; mix well. Cook covered for 15-20 minutes until tender.
Stir occasionally, until vegetables are tender and slightly browned.
recipes.tip: Roast vegetables first for deeper flavor and quicker cooking.

recipes.name: Light Shrimp Coconut Korma
steps: Sauté chopped onion until soft. Add ginger-garlic paste and korma spice blend, cook for 1 minute.
Stir in almond flour/paste and Greek yogurt, mix until smooth.
Add shrimp and light coconut milk, simmer gently for 5-7 minutes until shrimp are cooked through. Garnish with cilantro.
recipes.tip: Serve over steamed greens for a truly light meal.

recipes.name: Fiery Chicken Saag with Spinach
steps: Dice and brown chicken breast. Remove from pan. Sauté chopped onion, garlic, ginger, and green chilies.
Add diced tomatoes and garam masala; cook for 5 minutes. Stir in fresh spinach until wilted, then blend or finely chop into a paste.
Return chicken to the pan, add spinach paste and yogurt. Simmer for 10-15 minutes until chicken is tender.
recipes.tip: Adjust chili to your heat preference, enjoy!

recipes.name: Mushroom & Green Pea Masala
steps: Sauté chopped onion, ginger-garlic paste, cumin, and turmeric until fragrant.
Add sliced mushrooms and cook until softened. Stir in diced tomatoes and garam masala, simmer 5 mins.
Add frozen green peas and cook for another 5-7 minutes. Garnish with fresh cilantro.
recipes.tip: Add a squeeze of lemon for bright, fresh flavor.

recipes.name: Creamy Coconut Red Lentil Soup
steps: Sauté chopped onion, carrots, garlic, and ginger until softened.
Add red lentils, turmeric, and curry powder; stir for 1 minute.
Pour in vegetable broth and light coconut milk. Simmer for 20 minutes until lentils are tender, season to taste.
recipes.tip: Freeze extra portions for quick, nutritious lunches.

recipes.name: Spiced Lamb Kofta with Raita
steps: Combine ground lamb with finely minced onion, garlic, ginger, garam masala, and cumin. Form into small kofta balls.
Bake koftas at 400°F (200°C) for 15-20 minutes, or pan-fry until cooked through.
Mix Greek yogurt, diced cucumber, and chopped mint for the raita. Serve koftas with raita.
recipes.tip: Pair with a big salad for a complete, satisfying meal.

recipes.name: Curried Quinoa Vegetable Stir-fry
steps: Sauté chopped onion, garlic, and ginger until fragrant.
Add mixed vegetables and curry powder, stir-fry for 5-7 minutes until tender-crisp.
Stir in cooked quinoa, tamari, and vegetable broth. Cook until heated through.
recipes.tip: Use leftover quinoa for a super fast weeknight dinner.

recipes.name: Tangy Coconut Fish Curry
steps: Sauté chopped onion, garlic, ginger, and curry leaves until fragrant.
Add diced tomatoes, turmeric, and tamarind paste; cook for 5 minutes.
Pour in light coconut milk, bring to a simmer. Gently add fish fillets and cook for 8-10 minutes until fish is flaky.
recipes.tip: Serve with brown rice to soak up the delicious sauce.

recipes.name: Scrambled Paneer and Spinach Bhurji
steps: Sauté chopped onion, green chilies, and ginger-garlic paste.
Add diced tomatoes, turmeric, cumin, and coriander powder; cook until tomatoes soften.
Stir in crumbled paneer and fresh spinach. Cook until spinach wilts and paneer is heated through.
recipes.tip: Great for breakfast or a quick, high-protein lunch.

recipes.name: Smoky Roasted Eggplant Bharta
steps: Roast or grill whole eggplant until skin is charred and flesh is very soft. Peel and mash the pulp.
Sauté chopped onion, garlic, ginger, green chilies, cumin, and turmeric until fragrant.
Add mashed eggplant and diced tomatoes; simmer for 10-15 minutes. Garnish with fresh cilantro.
recipes.tip: For extra smoky flavor, grill eggplant directly over a flame.
